name: Jim's Teriyaki Marinade and BBQ Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Whisk together all ingredients except the corn starch.
2. Marinade meat for 1 hour (young chicken breasts or fish) or overnight (round steak) up to 5 days (some game cuts).
3. BBQ and baste with the marinade.
4. Make a slurry of the starch and water, then whisk into the marinade.
5. Bring to a boil and stir until the sauce thickens.
6. Serve with the BBQ'd meat.
Note: Real garlic and ginger enhance this dish. Adjust the amount of pepper sauce from mild to fiery. Substitute wine, sherry, orange or pineapple juice for some or all of the water to change the flavor. Pineapple juice adds an enzyme that further tenderizes the vinegar and mustard.
